items.title: LSS6 to boost renewable energy capacity, full commercial operation targeted by end-2029
items.summary: The Energy Transition and Water Transformation Ministry (Petra) said the programme will offer a total quota of 2,500 megawatts (MW) of solar capacity together with 1,250MW of Battery Energy Storage System (BESS), as well as 150MW of solar capacity dedicated to Bumiputera companies in Peninsular Malaysia. Package 1 comprises an open tender for all developers covering 2,200MW of solar capacity and 1,100MW of BESS capacity. Package 2 is an open tender for Bumiputera companies involving 300MW of solar capacity and 150MW of BESS, while Package 3 comprises 150MW of Bumiputera solar programme without BESS," it said in a statement today.

items.title: Burnham faces 'harder test' to mirror Manchester's revival
items.summary: WHEN Andy Burnham left Britain's parliament in 2017 to become Greater Manchester's first elected mayor, few would have predicted his return within a decade to be prime minister. "He played a good hand well," Henri Murison, chief executive at The Northern Powerhouse Partnership think tank, said of Burnham's mayoral tenure. On Friday, Burnham will vanquish the ghosts of his past to become ruling Labour's new leader.

items.title: Miri woman loses RM1.39m after clicking fake cake ad on social media
items.summary: MIRI, July 16 — A 43-year-old woman lost RM1,389,132 after falling victim to an elaborate scam that began with an online cake advertisement on social media. Miri police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) received a report on Wednesday on unauthorised withdrawals from the victim’s company bank accounts. After checking with the bank at around 10am on Wednesday, she discovered that 19 unauthorised fund transfers had been made from two of her company bank accounts to 15 different bank accounts the previous day, resulting in losses totalling RM1,389,132.

items.title: UK regulator to probe TikTok on child safety measures
items.summary: LONDON: Britain's media regulator on Thursday launched a probe into TikTok to understand whether its UK unit failed or is failing to protect children from harmful content. Ofcom will probe whether the platform has measures to assess if a particular user is a child and adequate systems and processes to prevent children from viewing harmful content. In May, the regulator said TikTok had failed to set out meaningful steps to protect British children from harmful online content.

items.title: PAC dissatisfied with response to KL land development recommendations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Parliamentary Public Accounts Committee (PAC) has expressed dissatisfaction with the government's responses to its recommendations on Kuala Lumpur land development, urging it to demonstrate genuine political will. PAC chairman Datuk Wira Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said the committee wants the government to reconsider its recommendations more seriously to show sincerity in resolving governance failures related to land management and development in the capital. Speaking at a press conference in Parliament today, she said the committee conducted a follow-up proceeding on July 15 to assess responses to the recommendations in the PAC Statement on Kuala Lumpur land development, which was tabled in the Dewan Rakyat on Aug 25 last year.

items.title: Govt to pilot affordable MediAsas health insurance plan this month ahead of nationwide rollout in 2027, says deputy minister
items.summary: Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the pilot programme would pave the way for the plan’s full implementation nationwide in January 2027. This will allow more Malaysians to access private healthcare protection,” he said during the question-and-answer session. He said BNM would introduce a no look-back policy under the MediAsas plan to ensure a fairer insurance market.

items.title: Man fined RM4,000 over false TikTok post on diesel shipment claim
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A man was fined RM4,000, in default six months' jail, by the Sessions Court here today for posting false content on TikTok about an alleged diesel shipment to the Philippines. The accused, Shahuddin Gowana, 51, pleaded guilty after the charge was read before Judge Monica Linsua. According to the charge, Shahuddin knowingly used the TikTok account "@izzyderabbit" to initiate the transmission of a communication containing false content with the intention of annoying others.

items.title: More than 3,600 Sabah citizenship applications still pending, Dewan Rakyat told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — As of May 31 this year, 3,640 citizenship applications in Sabah were still pending, while 10 had been approved and citizenship certificates issued to the successful applicants. Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said that, for late birth registration applications in Sabah, 2,659 applications had been approved, while 611 were still being processed. He said the Home Ministry (KDN) remained committed to expediting decisions on citizenship applications, particularly those involving late birth registration, through improvements to procedures, shorter waiting times and greater transparency in providing information to applicants.

items.title: Muhyiddin’s trial: Company’s RM800,000 donation to Bersatu linked to Jana Wibawa project, witness says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — KCJ Engineering Sdn Bhd’s RM800,000 donation in 2022 to political party Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia is related to a government project it had won under the Jana Wibawa programme, the High Court heard today. KCJ’s finance manager Zuriedah Deres@Musa said this while testifying as the 19th prosecution witness in former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s power abuse and money laundering trial in relation to Jana Wibawa. Today, Zuriedah said KCJ’s managing director Datuk Azman Yusoff had on September 1, 2022 instructed her to prepare the company’s cheques to donate RM800,000 to Bersatu.

items.title: Nearly 70pc of road accident victims last year were aged 16 to 40, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — A total of 69.4 per cent of all road accidents in the country last year involved people aged between 16 and 40. Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said there were 6,157 cases involving individuals aged 16 to 20, followed by 5,978 cases involving those aged 21 to 25, 4,716 cases involving those aged 26 to 30, and 3,640 cases involving individuals aged 31 to 35. “Heavy vehicles, drunk driving and reckless driving are among the main factors contributing to the high number of road accidents in the country,” he said during the oral question-and-answer session in the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Religious Affairs minister: Govt strengthens crackdown on online deviant teachings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — The government is adopting a whole-of-government approach to strengthen cooperation among agencies in efforts to contain the spread of deviant teachings, which are increasingly circulating on digital platforms and across borders. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), Zulkifli Hasan, said the government, together with the Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (JAKIM) and state Islamic religious authorities, remains vigilant in monitoring and addressing any deviant doctrines that go against the Ahli Sunnah Wal Jamaah school of thought. Zulkifli said the coordinated efforts of multiple agencies, including JAKIM, state Islamic religious departments, police, the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission, the National Security Council, local authorities, the Immigration Department and the National Registration Department, had enabled several enforcement actions.

items.title: Loke: Pakatan unfazed by possible BN-Perikatan cooperation in Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 16 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) says it is prepared for any political strategy by its rivals, including possible cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN), ahead of the Negeri Sembilan state election. DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said that, based on previous political developments, including during the Johor state election, PH would not be unsettled by any political manoeuvres by its opponents and would instead remain focused on strengthening its own election machinery. Whatever the challenges and competition, ultimately it is up to the people to decide,” he told reporters after officiating the 2026 Kampung Angkat Madani (Madani Adopted Village), Sekolah Angkat Madani (Madani Adopted School) and Santuni Madani programme organised by the Ministry of Transport in Kampung Mantin Dalam, near here, today.

items.title: Eleven held, including employer, after foreign worker dies from head injuries in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 16 — Johor police have arrested 11 suspects, including the victim’s employer, over the alleged murder of a foreign worker who was found covered in blood on a sidewalk in Taman Universiti here before dying in hospital days later. Johor Bahru North police chief Assistant Commissioner Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said authorities received a call from the public about the incident at 2.27am on June 28. “Following that, the victim was taken to Sultanah Aminah Hospital (HSA) Johor Bahru for treatment,” he said in a statement.

items.title: iCents bags RM12.9mil contract to renovate factory, office in Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: iCents Group Holdings Bhd's wholly-owned VC Engineering Sdn Bhd has secured a RM12.9 million contract to undertake renovation works for a factory, warehouse and office in the Klang Valley. "The scope of works comprises, among others, supply, delivery and installation of building works, internal finishes, mechanical, electrical, extra low voltage system, plumbing sanitary, fire fighting systems and testing commissioning works," iCents said. iCents executive director Foo Slang Leng said the contract broadens its project portfolio.

items.title: [UPDATED] Police must outpace evolving crime through AI, digitalisation, says deputy IGP
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The police force must stay ahead of evolving crime by harnessing digitalisation, data analytics and artificial intelligence (AI) to deliver more efficient and effective policing services. Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said policing today faced increasingly complex threats, including cybercrime, online scams, AI manipulation, deepfake technology and various forms of transnational crime. He said the force must stay ahead of criminal trends by embracing technology and innovation to strengthen policing capabilities.

items.title: Anwar mourns passing of scholar John L. Esposito, a ‘true friend to the Muslim world’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today extended his deepest condolences over the passing of Professor John L. Esposito, one of the world’s most respected scholars of Islam. In a Facebook post, Anwar said his friendship with Esposito spanned more than five decades, dating back to their first meeting in the early 1970s. “Esposito built bridges where others were content to build civilisational barriers,” the Prime Minister said.

items.title: PAC chairman: Committee to discuss possible probe into KWAP’s eFishery investment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — The Public Accounts Committee (PAC) will discuss whether it should conduct proceedings into the Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP)’s investment in start-up company eFishery, which has been linked to allegations of organised fraud. PAC chairman Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said the matter is not currently part of the committee’s proceedings. She said the PAC had conducted seven proceedings on UKM’s management, five proceedings on the R&D&C&I programme involving several ministries and agencies, while proceedings on PHB had begun and would continue next week.

items.title: Perak police bust alleged online scam syndicate, arrest 18 Chinese nationals
items.summary: IPOH, July 16 — Perak police have arrested 18 Chinese nationals in two separate raids around Ipoh over a suspected online scam operation believed to have exploited third-party financial accounts to defraud victims. Mohd Alwi said the suspects are aged between 26 and 45. Electronic devices allegedly used in a suspected online scam operation are displayed after Perak police arrested 18 Chinese nationals in two raids around Ipoh.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia higher at midday
items.summary: Among the heavyweights, Malayan Banking Bhd rose six sen to RM11.02, Public Bank Bhd added 12 sen to RM5.17, CIMB Group Holdings Bhd was one sen firmer at RM7.72, while Tenaga Nasional Bhd fell eight sen to RM14.30 and IHH Healthcare Bhd slid seven sen to RM8.55. As for the active stocks, NationGate Holdings Bhd gained 18 sen to RM1.15, VS Industry Bhd increased two sen to 24 sen, Dagang NeXchange Bhd rose three sen to 47.5 sen, while Tanco Holdings Bhd lost 1.5 sen to 30 sen and Thriven Global Bhd was down two sen to 10 sen. Of the top gainers, Petronas Dagangan Bhd increased 32 sen to RM19.64, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd rose 24 sen to RM29.34, United Plantations Bhd grew 20 sen to RM34.90, while IOI Properties Group Bhd firmed 15 sen to RM3.92. Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd led the losers list, falling 82 sen to RM44.92, while UMS Integration Ltd lost 30 sen to RM8.01, Kluang Rubber Co (Malaya) Bhd shed 17 sen to RM6.28, Panasonic Manufacturing Malaysia Bhd was 13 sen weaker at RM5.35, and Apollo Food Holdings Bhd eased 12 sen to RM5.73.

items.title: Family seeks RM21,500 for 4-year-old's pacemaker treatment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Four-year-old Wong Yong Cing has undergone medical procedures since infancy for a congenital heart condition that now requires a pacemaker. Wong said his daughter was born with a 4mm hole in her heart and had undergone several procedures since infancy. Congenital heart surgery medical officer at HSIS, Dr Hazwan Hairudin, said Wong Yong Ching was suffering from congenital heart disease involving a VSD.

items.title: Aminuddin says ‘not running away from Sikamat’ as he takes on BN stronghold Linggi in state polls
items.summary: “I am not running away from Sikamat,” he wrote. I love Sikamat. “Thank you, Sikamat,” he wrote.

items.title: DBKL rolls out 16 reforms after poor corruption ranking score
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DBKL's poor score in last year's Public Service Corruption Ranking has prompted 16 governance and administrative reforms to be implemented within six months, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the reforms were also driven by recommendations from a study by the International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM). She said DBKL scored just 0.08 per cent out of a possible five per cent in the ranking last year, highlighting the need for improvements in governance and administration.

items.title: China's rare earth curbs endanger US$6.5 trillion of Western industry, IEA says
items.summary: LONDON: The full implementation of China's rare earth export restrictions could put US$6.5 trillion of downstream production outside the country at risk, the International Energy Agency warned on Thursday. China, the world's largest producer of rare earths, expanded export controls in October last year to cover additional materials and introduced new licensing requirements, but later agreed to delay implementation for a year. Full implementation of the graphite controls could put about US$300 billion of downstream production outside China at risk, the report said.

items.title: Five teens detained over vandalism of Penampang Christian prayer site
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 16 — Police have detained five secondary school students in connection with the desecration of a Christian prayer site in Penampang. Penampang OCPD Superintendent Sammy Newton confirmed the arrests in a statement today. Instead, the investigation indicates the group was focused on mindless vandalism and destruction.

items.title: Woman charged with murder of newborn baby in Subang Jaya
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A supermarket saleswoman was charged at the magistrate's court today with murdering her newborn baby last week. Siti Nur Wahida Zainudin, who turned 23 on Tuesday, was accused of committing the offence between 5am and 5.30pm on July 7 at an apartment unit in USJ 14, Subang Jaya. Siti Nur Wahida, who remained expressionless throughout the proceedings, was unrepresented.

items.title: PAC wants FT minister back as KL land committee chairman, flags lack of MP input
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — The Public Accounts Committee (PAC) has urged the government to restore the Federal Territories minister’s role as chairman of the Kuala Lumpur Federal Territory Land Working Committee (JKTWPKL), saying the move would strengthen democratic accountability. PAC chairman Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said the committee was disappointed with the government’s response to its recommendations on Kuala Lumpur land development, particularly regarding the JKTWPKL chairmanship. Mas Ermieyati said PAC was also concerned over the lack of engagement with Members of Parliament on matters involving land approvals in Kuala Lumpur, describing it as a “deeply regrettable gap” in the governance process.

items.title: When suspicion becomes the default response
items.summary: THE recent hypermarket incident that went viral on social media was uncomfortable to watch. It is slowly worn down by countless small acts that many of us dismiss as harmless. Someone opens a punnet of blueberries, picks out the larger ones from another container to "improve" theirs, then quietly places the half-empty punnet back on the shelf for someone else to buy.

items.title: H5N1 bird flu could take hold in New Zealand within months
items.summary: Ministry for Primary Industries' Chief Veterinary Officer Mary van Andel told Radio New Zealand (RNZ) that eradication would be unlikely if the virus establishes in wildlife. The H5 virus was confirmed Wednesday in a seabird found on Petone Beach of the capital Wellington, marking New Zealand's first detection amid a global outbreak that has spread widely among wild birds. "New Zealand's isolation will not keep out this deadly bird virus," Professor Dianne Brunton at the University of Auckland School of Biological Sciences said, adding although there is no evidence of mass wildlife deaths or transmission between New Zealand birds, H5N1 is likely to become established in time.

items.title: Police: Military cosplay outfits may breach the law if it misleads public
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Wearing military uniforms and carrying imitation weapons at cosplay events could land participants in trouble with the law if they mislead the public or violate weapons regulations. Bukit Aman Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department director Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin said while wearing uniforms resembling security forces, including foreign soldiers, is not automatically an offence, it becomes one if done to mislead the public into believing the individual is an actual military member. Lman said organisers enforce compliance by prohibiting sharp objects or replica weapons that pose security risks.

items.title: Time Energy completes first phase of homeowner solar rewards programme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Time Energy Sdn Bhd has completed the first phase of its Green Rewards Programme, a community initiative that allows households with solar panels to earn renewable energy certificates (RECs). Time Energy is the renewable energy arm of Time dotCom Bhd. While solar RECs in Malaysia are commonly sourced from utility-scale renewable energy projects, the company said the Green Rewards Programme allows corporate buyers to source community-generated solar RECs from residential rooftops instead.

items.title: EU accepts Musk's proposed changes to X - for now
items.summary: BRUSSELS: Following a multimillion euro fine for breaches of EU rules, Elon Musk's online platform X has satisfied the European Commission with its proposed changes for now, the European Union said on Wednesday, reported German news agency dpa. The US company had been ordered to pay €120 million (US$137.7 million) due to a lack of transparency on X under the EU's Digital Services Act (DSA). In Brussels' view, this contravened EU rules.

items.title: Govt rules out age-based health checks for drivers aged 70 and above
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has no plans to impose mandatory health checks based solely on age for private motorists renewing their driving licences from the age of 70. Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said the ministry does not require additional medical examinations for the renewal of private driving licences, including for motorists aged 70 and above. He said research by the Malaysian Institute of Road Safety Research (Miros) found no conclusive evidence that such a policy would significantly reduce road accidents.

items.title: PAC: RM10.879b cooking oil subsidy failed to fully reach intended recipients
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — The Public Accounts Committee (PAC) has called for an overhaul of the country’s cooking oil subsidy management system after finding that the existing mechanism failed to ensure subsidised cooking oil reached those who genuinely needed it. PAC vice-chairman Teresa Kok said the committee’s report, prepared following findings in the Second Auditor-General’s Report 2025, identified weaknesses in the management of cooking oil price controls and subsidies under the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN), including ineffective targeting, breaches of sales conditions and supply chain management issues. Kok said the committee found that 1kg packet cooking oil was frequently misused by ineligible groups, including foreign nationals and commercial operators.

items.title: Hegseth announces testosterone screening for US troops
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth announced on Wednesday mandatory annual testosterone screening for troops 30 years old or above, an initiative he said was aimed at combatting testosterone deficiency that can undermine health. Hegseth said the screening could lead to troops being offered testosterone replacement therapy aimed at "ensuring you have the right testosterone levels to operate at your absolute best." The screening would become part of annual testing for troops aged 30 and above, and troops could voluntarily decide whether to accept any recommended hormone replacement treatment if they were diagnosed with low testosterone levels.

items.title: Police remind cosplayers to comply with laws on military uniforms, replica weapons
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Costumes representing anime, video game and movie characters have become a common sight at cosplay events organised around the capital. However, a few participants choose to wear Nazi and Imperial Japanese military uniforms from the Second World War era, complete with replica weapons to complement their costumes. Bukit Aman Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department director Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin said that wearing uniforms resembling those of security forces, including foreign soldiers, at cosplay events is not automatically an offence.

items.title: New York Times seeks to block subpoenas to reporters over Air Force One reporting
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The New York Times has filed a court motion to block federal grand jury subpoenas to three of its journalists over their reporting on President Donald Trump's new Qatari-donated Air Force One, a spokesperson said on Wednesday. Jay Clayton, the US Attorney in Manhattan, issued the subpoenas on Friday, shortly after the New York Times reported that the new Air Force One lacked some of the security features of the older aircraft. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: PN rejects Bersatu claim over postponed seat talks
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has dismissed Bersatu's claim that the coalition's Seat Negotiation Committee meeting was postponed indefinitely because Pas wanted to pursue political cooperation with Barisan Nasional (BN). PN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the postponement was linked to a complaint lodged with the Registrar of Societies (ROS) by a Bersatu member questioning the legitimacy of the party's leadership. Muhyiddin said the decision was made last night after the PN Seat Negotiation Committee meeting was postponed indefinitely, with Bersatu excluded from seat allocation discussions.

items.title: DBKL implements 16 reforms after poor corruption ranking score, Dewan Rakyat told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has implemented 16 governance and administrative reform initiatives over the past six months after receiving a low score in the Public Service Corruption Ranking under the 2025 Local Authority Star Rating System. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said that DBKL scored only 0.08 per cent out of the allocated 5 per cent for the assessment. She said that, based on the findings of a study conducted by the International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM) following an engagement session with Members of Parliament (MPs) in the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur on March 2, four key recommendations were identified to strengthen DBKL’s administration, governance, integrity, and service delivery system.

items.title: Penang Port introduces new SOP for ship waste management to strengthen green efforts
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 16 — The Penang Port Commission (PPC) has implemented a new Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for the management of ship-generated waste at Penang Port as part of efforts to strengthen environmental protection and support the port’s Green Port Policy. “This port circular has been issued to set out the SOP, ensuring uniformity, compliance with relevant laws and smooth operations in handling ship-generated waste at Penang Port,” he said in a statement today. He explained that the SOP categorises ship-generated waste into three groups, namely scheduled waste from ships, domestic waste (including dunnage) and sewage waste from ships.

items.title: UN: More than 500 feared dead after boats sink off Myanmar coast
items.summary: BANGKOK: Two boats carrying more than 500 people may have capsized off the coast of Myanmar in recent days, UN agencies said on Thursday, as refugees from the war-torn country continue to make perilous maritime journeys in search of safety and better opportunities. "According to preliminary information, the two vessels departed from Myanmar's Rakhine State in late June carrying mostly Rohingya passengers, reportedly including some who had travelled from refugee camps in Cox's Bazar, Bangladesh," the International Organisation for Migration and the UN Refugee Agency said in a joint statement, saying more than 500 were feared dead. With around 250 people onboard, the first boat lost contact shortly after departure and a second vessel carrying around 280 people is believed to have sunk off Myanmar's Ayeyarwady coast on July 8, the UN agencies said.

items.title: Mat Sabu: Measures in place to protect food supply from El Nino impact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — The government is strengthening various mitigation measures to reduce the impact of the El Nino phenomenon on the agro-food sector, including ensuring more efficient water resource management, expanding the use of smart irrigation technology and providing assistance to affected farmers and rice farmers. Minister of Agriculture and Food Security Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said among the measures implemented were continuous monitoring of water levels in dams and catchment areas, integrated water resource management, the use of smart irrigation monitoring systems and drip irrigation systems to optimise water use. He said the government also provides protection and assistance schemes for rice farmers affected by disasters including drought.

items.title: Dzulkefly: Type 2 diabetes now affects Malaysians as young as 14
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Type 2 diabetes is now being diagnosed in Malaysians as young as 14, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said. The minister said data from the National Diabetes Registry showed that new cases among people aged below 30 had increased from 1,926 in 2024 to 1,932 in 2025, with 660 new cases already recorded as of June this year. Dzulkefly said figures from the Health Ministry's National Health Screening Initiative, which screened 347,813 people aged between 18 and 30 in 2025, painted an equally worrying picture.

items.title: Transforming Malaysia's school counselling units
items.summary: THE time has come for the school counselling units in Malaysia to reassess their roles and positions. However, contemporary challenges, including rising mental health concerns, bullying, and the pressing demand for employability skills, call for a decisive and strategic repositioning of the Malaysian school counselling unit. This initiative introduced and piloted the Human and Career Development Centre (HCDC) model designed to support the school counselling unit under the Malaysian Education Blueprint 2013–2025 (MEB 2013-2025).

items.title: Education Ministry: Nearly 20pc of Chinese secondary students enrolled in UEC schools this year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Nearly 20 per cent of Chinese secondary school students in Malaysia are enrolled in Chinese independent high schools offering the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) stream this year, according to the Education Ministry. In a written parliamentary reply dated yesterday, the ministry said 75,144 Chinese students are studying in UEC stream schools, accounting for 19.4 per cent of the total 387,406 Chinese secondary school students nationwide. It said 260,699 Chinese students, or 67.3 per cent, are enrolled in secondary schools under the Education Ministry, while the remaining 51,563 students, or 13.3 per cent, are studying in private education institutions.

items.title: Europe Inc heads into strongest earnings season in years, but AI gap persists
items.summary: Reuters LONDON/GDANSK: European companies are heading for their strongest earnings season in more than three years, but investors remain concerned that the region lacks enough artificial intelligence (AI)-powered growth engines to keep pace with the United States. Second-quarter profits of European blue-chip companies are expected to grow by 15.3 per cent on average, the most since the last quarter of 2022, according to LSEG I/B/E/S data. Earnings at US companies are forecast to grow 23.7 per cent on average.

items.title: Businessman loses RM1.25mil to cryptocurrency scam
items.summary: IPOH: A businessman has lost RM1.25 million after falling victim to a cryptocurrency investment scam that promised lucrative returns. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the police received a report from the victim on July 10. He said initial investigations found that the scam began in January last year when the victim was introduced to several individuals offering a cryptocurrency investment scheme with promises of high returns.

items.title: Cops bust alleged online scam syndicate, arrest 18 Chinese citizens
items.summary: IPOH: Police have arrested 18 Chinese citizens in two raids here for allegedly operating an online scam syndicate. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the suspects, all men, were detained during operations conducted by the district police headquarters on Monday. Alwi said police view online scam activities involving the abuse of technology and the misuse of financial accounts seriously.

items.title: US imposes 25pct tariff on some goods from Brazil
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US plans to impose a 25 per cent tariff on certain imports from Brazil under Section 301 of the Trade Act, US Trade Representative (USTR) Jamieson Greer said late on Wednesday. The action follows a year-long probe which concluded that Brazilian policies on digital trade, tariffs, intellectual property, ethanol access and deforestation burden US commerce, USTR said in a statement. Brazil would be the first country targeted under US President Donald Trump's new tariff strategy which relies on Section 301 of US trade law, a provision that authorises investigations into alleged unfair trade practices.

items.title: PM directs Mardi to speed up development of high-yield, drought-resistant padi varieties
items.summary: Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim issued the directive during a national food security meeting yesterday. He said Mardi had been instructed to intensify its research, particularly into padi varieties capable of producing significantly higher yields per hectare. Mohamad said Mardi had developed several padi varieties, including the newly introduced MR333 Menora, as well as MR297, MR315, MR307, MR284 and MRQ76.

items.title: Sarawak brewery 1602 cleared of wrongdoing after police investigation, seized items returned
items.summary: KUCHING, July 16 — Sarawak brewery 1602 has been cleared of any wrongdoing following the completion of a police investigation, with all seized products and merchandise returned to the company. He said all beer products, merchandise and related items seized during the probe had since been returned, allowing the company to resume normal business operations. Lee said the company had fully cooperated with authorities throughout the investigation by providing all relevant documents and information requested.

items.title: Full report on fatal grenade blast at Hobart Camp expected on July 23
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The probe into the grenade blast that killed two soldiers at Hobart Camp in Kedah last month has yet to be concluded. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Khaled Nordin told the Dewan Rakyat that the Defence Ministry is expecting a complete report from the army headquarters on July 23, 2026. He said a Board of Inquiry had convened a day after the incident on June 17, and the investigation report had since gone through reviews at the 2nd Division Headquarters and the Army Western Field Command Headquarters.

items.title: Mindef: Military can conduct exercises in states without royal consent
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Defence Minister Datuk Seri Khaled Nordin said in Parliament today that he agrees with the argument that military exercises conducted in Pahang do not require royal consent. Ikmal said this power is clearly outlined in the Federal Constitution, to which Khaled agreed. Among the explanations in his own question, he said the Federal Constitution clearly outlines Federal and State powers,” Khaled said.

items.title: Perlis RTD crackdown finds four commercial vehicle workers test positive for drugs
items.summary: KANGAR, July 16 — The Perlis Road Transport Department (RTD) has seized the Competent Driving Licence (CDL) and Vocational Licence (GDL) belonging to a commercial vehicle driver who tested positive for drugs in an Integrated Drug Operation on commercial vehicles. Perlis RTD director Noraini Mohd Nawi said that since the operation began on July 1 until yesterday, a total of 71 commercial vehicle drivers underwent urine screening by the National Anti-Drug Agency (AADK), with the results finding one driver and three attendants tested positive for drugs. Noraini said the operation was to strengthen compliance with the provisions of the traffic regulations, improve the level of safety for road users and reduce the risk of accidents involving commercial vehicles, especially due to drivers under the influence of drugs.

items.title: Teenage girl missing from Pandan Jaya Flats since Monday, say police
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Police are seeking the public’s assistance to locate a teenage girl who has been reported missing from her home at the Pandan Jaya Flats here since Monday (July 13). Ampang Jaya police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said that Norziah Jorhan, 17, is believed to have left her home at about 5 am. Those with information can contact Insp Nur Alia Dyana Raimi at 017-6515201 or the Ampang Jaya Police Headquarters Control Centre at 03-42897222 or go to the nearest police station.

items.title: Shut out of Johor, Bersama opts to sit out Negeri Sembilan election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) has announced it will sit out the upcoming Negeri Sembilan state election, choosing instead to redirect its resources toward the Melaka state election expected later this year. In a statement yesterday, Bersama leaders Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad and Syukri Razab said the party would not field candidates when nomination day opens in Negeri Sembilan this Saturday. “Taking these issues into account, along with constraints of time and resources, Bersama believes it is more strategic to shift its focus to the Melaka state election,” the party said, adding that its “full focus” would now go toward Melaka.

items.title: Anwar says 200-litre BUDI95 quota fits most drivers as average RON95 use is 100 litres
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim defended the reduction of the BUDI95 fuel quota from 300 litres to 200 litres a month, saying the move was based on actual usage data showing that most users consumed far less than the new limit. Anwar, who is also the finance minister, said fewer than 1 per cent of BUDI95 users consistently consumed more than 200 litres of RON95 between October 2025 and May 2026. Anwar said the eligibility limit was reduced from 300 litres to 200 litres after considering actual consumption patterns, while balancing fuel supply security with the need to maintain targeted assistance for the people.

items.title: Bukit Aman: Sarawak teen arrested at Hong Kong airport over alleged 3kg cannabis haul
items.summary: According to New Straits Times , Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said Hong Kong authorities had informed Malaysian police of the arrest after the teenager arrived at Hong Kong International Airport on July 9. Hong Kong Customs found three kilogrammes of cannabis buds in the suspect’s possession. He arrived at Hong Kong International Airport on July 9,” Hussein said.

items.title: MACC, Customs propose task force to monitor port enforcement and tax collection
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 16 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) and the Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) are proposing the establishment of a special task force to monitor enforcement and tax collection at the country’s strategic ports. MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the proposal was discussed during a courtesy visit by JKDM director-general Datuk Amran Ahmad to the MACC headquarters here yesterday. Also present were MACC Investigation Division senior director Datuk Mohd Hafaz Nazar and JKDM Integrity branch head Azian Umar.

items.title: KWAP deceived into RM200m eFishery investment despite due diligence, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said the Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP) was deceived by Indonesian aquaculture startup eFishery after the company manipulated its financial statements, despite the fund carrying out due diligence before investing about RM200 million. “The investment in eFishery also involved several leading institutional investors and international technology funds, including Temasek, SoftBank, 42XFund and Northstar. “These investors also have internationally recognised investment assessment, control and governance processes,” he said.

items.title: Girl, 13, dies after firefighters pull two teens from blazing Kuching home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — A 13-year-old girl died after firefighters rescued her from a blazing terraced house in Kuching early today, but she could not be saved despite emergency treatment at the scene. “Initial information indicated that two victims were still inside the house when the fire broke out,” the statement said. Firefighters entered the burning house and rescued both teenagers before administering first aid at the scene and handing them over to Ministry of Health personnel.
